title: MCAD: Deadline for Filing a Complaint of Discrimination (300-Day Rule)
description: MCAD page setting out the statute of limitations for discrimination complaints under M.G.L. c.151B: file within 300 days of the last discriminatory act. Explains three exceptions — CBA-grievance tolling (300 days from when the employee knew/should have known the grieved matter supports a discrimination claim), delayed notice, and equitable tolling for incapacity — each decided case-by-case by the Investigating Commissioner.
notes: KEY DEADLINE: 300 days from the last discriminatory act (c.151B). CBA-grievance exception (300 days from when the employee knew/should have known the grievance supports a discrimination claim) is directly relevant to unionized DDS/MassAbility employees. Missing the period may permanently bar the claim.
